name: AG2
description: AG2 — Community-maintained fork of AutoGen providing multi-agent conversation and orchestration framework.

seo.title: AG2 — Code Assistants / Agent Tooling
seo.meta_description: AG2: AG2 — Community-maintained fork of AutoGen providing multi-agent conversation and orchestration framework.

when_to_use: Orchestrating multi-agent conversations for complex task solving
Building AutoGen-compatible agents with community-maintained fork
Running collaborative LLM agent pipelines for coding automation
Experimenting with agent conversation patterns and tool use

pros: Community-maintained AutoGen fork with active governance
Proven multi-agent conversation patterns out of the box
Python-native, integrates with common LLM providers
Open source with clearer licensing than original AutoGen
cons: Fragmented ecosystem confusion with Microsoft AutoGen
Documentation still catching up with upstream divergence
Agent orchestration abstractions can feel heavy for simple tasks
review.verdict: A credible community fork of AutoGen, but the split ecosystem means you pick a side and accept the churn.
review.best_for: Python teams building multi-agent systems who prefer community governance
review.not_for: Teams that want simple single-agent workflows or vendor-backed stability

developer_experience.reason: Python multi-agent framework, docs improving, community-led
